name: Pickled or Corned Beef or Venison
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat into 4-6 pound pieces.
2. Prepare a barrel and spread a layer of salt on the bottom.
3. Rub each piece of meat with a mixture of salt and pepper, then pack it down in layers, covering each layer with more salt.
4. Ensure the top layer is covered with salt.
5. Allow the meat to stand overnight.
6. In the morning, prepare the brine by dissolving all ingredients in 2 gallons of water. Stir until fully dissolved; test with an egg—if it floats, the mixture is correct; if not, add more salt.
7. Pour the brine over the packed salted meat, adding more water to cover the meat if necessary.
8. Cover the barrel tightly and place a heavy weight on top to prevent floating.
9. Use or store the pickled meat for up to 2-3 weeks. For 100 pounds of meat, double all ingredients.
